channel.title: Politics-Uk on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Politics-Uk on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Protesters In Southampton Use Institutional Sympathy Language, Surprised When Police Crack Their Heads Anyway
item.description:
SOUTHAMPTON — Police have reportedly proceeded to mass arrests and beatings after protesters outside Southampton Police Station deployed familiar anti-racist slogans.

The crowd gathered outside the station chanting “racist police off our streets” and “I can’t breathe”, in what witnesses described as a bold attempt to activate the moral force field normally generated when those phrases are used by university students, NGOs, activist collectives or people standing beside professionally printed placards.

item.title: UK: Pensioner Arrested For Saying Government Should Not Give Legal Exemptions To Religious Minorities Carrying Ceremonial Weapons After Man Uses Ceremonial Energised Plasma Rifle To Level Building
item.description:
LONDON — A pensioner has been arrested on suspicion of religious hatred after saying the government should not give legal exemptions to religious minorities carrying ceremonial weapons, days after a man legally carrying a ceremonial energised plasma rifle was convicted of using one to level a building, killing 12 people and injuring 28 others.

Harold Davis, 74, reportedly made the comment outside a newsagent while watching a televised debate in which politicians, police officials, community leaders and sensitivity advisers stressed that the incident should not be used to question the broader exemption framework.
